author: @CaseyVan
text: That is very clever. I had an idea of making a basic 5 string headless slide guitar that costs almost nothing and is made of parts from a hardware store, and nothing more than drilling holes in wood is required. For the tuners, I was thinking of a dual tuner system. At the head side of the neck, an array of ordinary screws for tuners. Since we know these can slide, I thought of just using a single metal plate that you can screw down to make a locking head. Then on the bridge side, I wanted to come up with fine tuners. So the idea is to install the strings, then tune them slightly low on the head, then tighten down the metal plate. Then on the bridge side use the fine tuners. The fine tuners would tighten down the strings further by simply pulling the strings against the body. So all that would be needed would be 5 screws for the headstock tuners, the plate with maybe two screws, and the fine tuning screws possibly just the screw alone holding the strings down. I had some other ideas but I don't want to mention them until I have completed this design. So basically a block of wood with some kind of primitive nut on one end and a simple saddle on the end.

author: @Jonnybristol100
text: Try 4 mm Allen key bolts instead of slotted bolts, they are a smaller and then you wont have to grind down the head and the heads are easier turn with an Allen key when the strings are tightened.

author: @mijodo2008
text: Robert, you sir, are an absolute GENIUS. That has got to be the smartest, most compact string tensioner I have ever seen. Your use of inexpensive and relatively easy to aquire items, altered and reconfigured to do the job of string tensioning in a small space is just what I have been looking for. I will build a similar item, using yours for inspiration. Thanks mate :) Top job and many kind regards.
